target_inquiry: effect of city platform exposure on firm green patent count
rival_claim: Generic digitalization exposure could be the relevant concept rather than auditable platform rollout exposure.
explains_well: It explains broad digital-government narratives that do not depend on exact rollout dates.
explains_poorly: It does not support a unit-level treatment concept when platform timing and linkage are the intended design objects.
discriminating_observation: Check whether source rows and design declarations identify specific rollout timing instead of broad digitalization language.
evidence_needed: Source locators for rollout timing, treatment definition, and unit linkage.

target_inquiry: effect of city platform exposure on firm green patent count
rival_claim: Baseline city administrative capacity explains both earlier platform rollout and stronger innovation outcomes.
explains_well: It explains why high-capacity cities might adopt platforms early and show stronger patenting regardless of treatment.
explains_poorly: It does not explain within-city service-friction changes unless those changes are directly observed after rollout.
discriminating_observation: Compare pre-rollout innovation and administrative-capacity trends against post-rollout service-process changes.
evidence_needed: Pre-trend diagnostics, baseline capacity measures, and source evidence on service process changes.
